text: I assist you ? What answer could he possibly give? He’d searched after it for nearly two centuries and still had no satisfying answer . They reached the gate into the Bot District. This one was also open, but only wide enough to allow people through single-file. Large flood lights atop the wall bathed the area in harsh white light . The timing of their arrival was convenient . “No more conversation. I’ll talk if they ask anything,” he said . Two gearheads stood guard, one on each side of the gap. Their weapons were more formidable than those of the bots at the roadblock on the edge of town. These were pre-Black Out assault rifles, built with matching parts. Even without armor-piercing rounds, they had a chance of penetrating Ronin’s casing from this range . Lara fell behind Ronin, following close on his heels. The gearheads’ optics flickered from her to Ronin. Perhaps he should have had the foresight to remove his own rifle from its protective carrier before coming here . “Dustwalker,” one of the gearheads — a synth — said in greeting. The right sleeve of his coat had been removed, displaying the bare blue casing of his arm, and Warlord’s mark on his shoulder . “Cobalt.” Ronin nodded. He slowed to a stop several feet away from the gearheads. Lara didn’t bump into him this time, but pressed lightly against his back. His skin flared to life, electrodes firing off a wave of pleasure at her touch despite the clothing separating them . “Heard you had a chat with Warlord last night,” the other bot said. It was a squat model with a battered, blocky casing. Went by the name Northside . Lara stiffened and inhaled sharply . They didn’t do anything to me . Why did she react like that at mention of Ronin meeting Warlord ? “ I did .” “Convinced you to sample some local goods?” asked Cobalt . Heat pulsed off Lara. She shifted, as though to move around him; simulations suggested she was likely to go into one of her outbursts . Ronin reached back, placing a hand on her hip, and glanced back at her over his shoulder. She stared at him with jaw clenched and eyes ablaze, but didn’t move . “There is an agreement in place,” he said, loud enough for the gearheads to hear . She eased back, tension draining from her body. Volatile things, humans. But hadn’t Ronin reacted in similar fashion to Warlord and his gearheads ? “Go on in with her, then. Creators know I’d be needing something like that after being in the Dust as long as you were .” The gearheads stepped aside. Lara fell into step immediately behind Ronin as he walked. They were almost there; through the gates and less than half a mile to his residence . “Send word when you’re done with her,” Cobalt said. “I wouldn’t mind a go. Not many of them have hair like that .” A twitch skittered over the palm of Ronin’s hand, fingers curling as though to grip his rifle. How many gearheads served Warlord? Ronin needed to know how many bullets to stockpile . He led Lara through the narrow opening, crossing the threshold from her world into what should have been his . Ahead of them, the broad street — lined by orange streetlights — curved away to the southeast. Directly north lay the park, grass and leaves glistening with moisture in the artificial light. It was one of the greenest places Ronin had seen since waking. Yet there’d never been anyone save the maintenance bots within, trimming the vegetation . It seemed a waste . There were only a few brown patches in the grass, and it thrived in many spots thanks to the shade of the trees ringing the park. He could just make out the water of the central pond through the trunks, reflecting the glow of Cheyenne’s lights on the clouds . “They are not touching me,” Lara hissed from behind. “And what the hell did you and Warlord talk about ?” Ronin turned to face her, scanning the empty street and the imposing, eclectic wall. Though none were in sight, there were gearheads all over Cheyenne, and they all reported faithfully to their leader . “This way. Shouldn’t be talking about that out here .” “I’m not moving another step.” She folded her arms across her chest. “Tell me. Why did you have a chat with that…that thing ?” She’d made her loathing of bots clear when Ronin first met her, but this was somehow different. Though she didn’t raise her voice, venom dripped from every word . Here, in the middle of the main road leading to the heart of the Bot District. Here, where gearheads were likely to walk by at any moment . Ronin stepped close to her, tilting his head down to meet her gaze. Every trip into the Dust put him in danger, but he had no desire to tempt deactivation here , now . “This is not the place to speak of these things, Lara Brooks .” “Well, I’m not going with you. Food be damned. I won’t be a part of any plans that involve that thing .” She turned and walked back toward the gate . “I’m not going to follow you back through. When they see you alone …” “I’d prefer a bullet in the back .” “We both know it won’t be a bullet .” Lara froze. She hung her head, water streaming from her wrap and the stray strands of her hair. Her free hand trembled in a fist at her side. Whatever wounds she’d suffered were still fresh . He closed the distance between them. She didn’t move away . “I’ll tell you what you want to know as soon as we’re secure in my residence,” he said, softening his voice . She turned her head, angling her ear toward him . “Warlord
